summary: Added and wired several reproducible tooling/environment pieces: a Nix-packaged devkitPPC 46.1 cross toolchain, a newlib patch needed for gcc-cross compilation, a Nix-packaged m2c from matt-kempster/m2c, extra Python/decomp shell dependencies, and default m2c invocation flags for K&R-style output with left pointer binding. The decomp.dev bot reported no GALE01 changes, so this was infrastructure-only rather than a matching-output change.
what_changed: Added a Nix overlay entry for `devkitppc` in `.nix/default.nix` via `super.callPackage ./devkitppc.nix {}`.

Added `.nix/devkitppc.nix`, packaging devkitPPC version `46.1` using `pkgsCross.ppc-embedded` GCC 14 and binutils, with devkitPro patch URLs fetched from the matching `devkitPPC_r46.1` buildscripts tag.
Wrapped `powerpc-none-eabi-*` binaries as `powerpc-eabi-*` in the devkitPPC derivation, matching tool names expected by existing build/decomp workflows.
Patched Nix `newlib` in `.nix/default.nix` by appending `.nix/newlib.patch` to fix gcc-cross compilation.
Added `.nix/newlib.patch`, which injects missing extern declarations in several `libgloss/rs6000` files for functions such as `write`, `read`, `exit`, `brk`, `__pcrlf`, `__outln`, and `__outstr`.
Added `.nix/m2c.nix`, packaging `matt-kempster/m2c` at commit `4caccf86a027bf52b9132a8ae9331cb3210305a8` as a Python package with `graphviz`, `poetry-core`, and `pycparser` dependencies.
Extended `.nix/shell.nix` devshell inputs with Nix-packaged `m2c`, `pyelftools`, and `pcpp`.
Updated `tools/decomp.py` so generated m2c commands include `--knr --pointer left` before targeting `ppc-mwcc-c`.
decomp.dev bot comment reported `No changes` for GALE01, indicating the tooling updates did not affect the generated game binary in this PR.
smart_moves: Kept the devkitPPC integration reproducible by pinning version `46.1`, using fixed-output hashes for fetched patches, and deriving patch URLs from the devkitPro buildscripts tag.
Used Nix `overrideAttrs` to append patches to existing GCC, binutils, and newlib derivations rather than replacing whole packages.
Used `makeWrapper` to provide compatibility binary names from `powerpc-none-eabi-*` to `powerpc-eabi-*`, avoiding broader script churn.
Packaged m2c inside the Nix shell instead of relying on an ambient global Python install, improving dev environment consistency.
Made m2c defaults explicit in `tools/decomp.py` so future invocations consistently use K&R output and left pointer style for the `ppc-mwcc-c` target.
Validated that the infrastructure-only PR produced no GALE01 output changes before merge.
decomp_lessons: Tooling PRs can be high leverage even with no source/matching changes: reproducible compiler, binutils, m2c, and Python dependencies reduce setup drift across contributors.
When adding cross-compilers through Nix, keep compatibility with established tool names expected by build scripts; wrapper derivations can bridge naming differences cleanly.
For decomp helper tools like m2c, codify preferred style flags in the wrapper script instead of expecting contributors to remember them manually.
Patching upstream Nix packages with small, targeted patches can unblock cross-compilation while keeping the rest of the package definition inherited from nixpkgs.
Use automated decomp.dev/no-output-change reports as evidence that toolchain/devshell changes did not perturb the target binary.
naming_conventions: `devkitppc` was added as the Nix package/overlay name, matching the devkitPPC toolchain concept while using lowercase Nix attribute style.
The devkitPPC derivation wraps `powerpc-none-eabi-*` executables under shorter `powerpc-eabi-*` names.
m2c is packaged as a Nix/Python package with `pname = "m2c"` and `version = "unstable"` because the source is pinned by commit rather than a release tag.
The devkitPro tag format used in the derivation is `devkitPPC_r${version}`, concretely `devkitPPC_r46.1`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added default m2c invocation flags `--knr --pointer left`, likely to produce C syntax closer to the style expected by MWCC-oriented decomp workflows; the diff directly shows these flags added to `tools/decomp.py`.
Kept the m2c target as `ppc-mwcc-c`, reinforcing that generated C should be tuned for PowerPC/MWCC matching work.
No direct assembly matching, symbol matching, or source decompilation changes are evidenced in this PR.
The bot-reported `No changes` for GALE01 is the main matching-related evidence: the tooling update did not change the built target output.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only comment was an automated decomp.dev report: `Report for GALE01 ... No changes`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rely on `.nix/devkitppc.nix` or the `devkitppc` nativeBuildInput.
Find PRs modifying `tools/decomp.py` m2c flags after `--knr --pointer left` was added.
Find Nix/toolchain PRs that update devkitPPC version, GCC/binutils patches, or newlib.patch.
Find decomp work where m2c output style or pointer placement affected matching cleanup.
